title: Dagster Component Schema Specification
description: Master specification for all component schema.json files in dagster-component-templates. A UI editor reads this file to understand the valid structure, field types, widgets, and I/O contracts for every component.

componentSchema.description: The structure every component's schema.json must follow

description.description: One-sentence human-readable description shown in the component picker.

category.description: Functional category — controls folder, I/O contract, and UI grouping.

icon.description: Lucide icon name to display in the UI. See 'icons' section for valid values.

x-dagster-io.description: Declares the data I/O contract for this component. Drives port display and type-checking in the canvas editor.

attributes.description: Maps each configurable field name to its field descriptor. The UI editor renders a form from this.

tags.description: Search/filter tags shown in the component picker.

x-dagster-provides.description: Resource keys this component will register in Definitions.resources when configured. Enables the designer to: (1) populate resource-picker dropdowns from project components, (2) validate that resource_key references have a provider, (3) render resource dependency edges in the pipeline graph.

ioContract.description: Specifies what data types flow in and out of a component. Used by the canvas editor to validate connections between components.

inputs.description: Describes the primary input port. Omit if the component has no DataFrame input.

type.description: 'dataframe' is the most common — the component receives a pandas DataFrame via the Dagster IO manager.

required.description: Whether an upstream connection must be present.

description.description: Tooltip shown on the input port.

outputs.description: Describes the primary output port. Omit if the component produces no downstream-consumable output.

type.description: 'dataframe' = passes a pandas DataFrame downstream via IO manager. 'MaterializeResult' = terminal sink/ingestion, returns metadata only.

description.description: Tooltip shown on the output port.

fieldDescriptor.description: Describes one configurable attribute. The UI editor uses this to render the correct input widget and validate values.

type.description: JSON Schema primitive type for this field.

label.description: Human-readable field label shown above the input widget.

description.description: Help text shown below the input widget. Should explain what the field does and give an example if non-obvious.

required.description: If true, the UI shows this field as required and validates it is non-empty before saving.

default.description: Default value pre-filled in the widget. Can be string, number, boolean, array, object, or null.

enum.description: Restricts valid values to this list. When present, the UI renders a 'select' dropdown (or 'radio' for ≤3 short values).

items.description: For type='array', describes each element. Typically {"type": "string"} or {"type": "object"}.

ui:widget.description: Override the auto-selected widget. See 'widgets' section for valid values and when to use each.

widgets.description: All valid ui:widget values, when they are auto-selected, and when to manually set them.

text.description: Single-line text input. Auto-selected for string fields. Suitable for names, keys, short values.
text.autoSelected: string fields without a special name suffix

textarea.description: Multi-line text area. Auto-selected when field name contains: description, prompt, template, message, text, content.

code.description: Code editor (monospace, syntax highlighting). Auto-selected when field name contains: query, sql, expression, condition, formula, code, script.

select.description: Dropdown selector. Auto-selected when 'enum' is present with more than 3 options.

radio.description: Radio button group. Use for 'enum' with 2-3 short option labels.

checkbox.description: Boolean toggle/checkbox. Auto-selected for boolean fields.

number.description: Numeric spinner input. Auto-selected for integer and number fields.

list.description: Dynamic list of strings. Auto-selected for array fields with string items. Each row is one string value.

key-value.description: Dynamic key-value editor. Auto-selected for object fields. Each row is one key:value pair.

file.description: File path picker (opens OS file dialog). Use for fields that specify a local file or directory path.

password.description: Masked input. Auto-selected for fields that contain credentials. Stored encrypted in vault.
password.autoSelected: fields named password, secret, api_token, access_token, auth_token, or ending in _password/_secret/_token

resource-picker.description: Resource reference picker. Renders a dropdown of existing Dagster resources already registered in the project, with a 'Create new resource' link that navigates to the Resources tab. The selected value is stored as the resource key string. When no resources exist yet, shows only the 'Create new' prompt.
resource-picker.autoSelected: fields named resource_key or ending in _resource_key

uiBehavior.emptyState: Show 'No resources defined yet' + 'Create a resource →' link to Resources tab
uiBehavior.createLink: Opens Resources tab > New Resource form, pre-filtered to compatible resource types where possible
uiBehavior.storedValue: string — the resource key, e.g. 's3_resource' or 'my_snowflake'

ioContractsByCategory.description: Standard I/O contracts by category. All components in a category should conform to the same contract so they are interchangeable in the canvas editor.

resource.description: Resource-providing components register a Dagster resource, no asset I/O

icons.description: Icon name for this component. Two icon sets are supported: Lucide (default, geometric UI icons) and Simple Icons (brand/technology logos). Prefix Simple Icons with "si:" — e.g. "si:snowflake", "si:mongodb", "si:postgresql". Lucide icons use no prefix — e.g. "Database", "Filter", "BarChart2". Browse Lucide at lucide.dev and Simple Icons at simpleicons.org.

lucide.description: Geometric UI icons. Use for generic components (transforms, analytics, AI) where no brand logo applies.

simpleicons.description: Brand and technology logos. Use for components that connect to a specific technology or SaaS product.

componentRegistry.description: All component categories and their folder locations within the repository.
componentRegistry.manifestDescription: Full registry of all components with paths and GitHub raw URLs. Updated when new components are added.

parsingInstructions.description: How a UI editor should load and parse components from this repository.
steps: 1. Fetch manifest.json to get the list of all components and their paths.
2. For each component, load its schema.json using the path from the manifest.
3. Validate the schema.json against the 'componentSchema' definition in this file.
4. Use 'category' to determine the I/O ports (see ioContractsByCategory).
5. Use 'attributes' to build the configuration form — render each field using its 'type', 'ui:widget', and 'enum'.
6. Use 'x-dagster-io' for canvas port display and connection type-checking.
7. Use 'icon' and 'tags' for the component picker UI.
8. When rendering a field: if 'ui:widget' is present, use it; otherwise auto-select based on 'type' and field name per the 'widgets' section.

connectors.description: Explicitly declares which connector ports are shown in the canvas editor. The UI reads this directly — do not infer from x-dagster-io.
connectors.left: Whether this component can receive an upstream input connection (left port). False = no left connector shown.
connectors.right: Whether this component can provide an output that downstream components can depend on (right port). False = no right connector shown.

ingestion.note: No upstream DataFrame; right port = MaterializeResult (row count / metadata) for downstream dependency edges, not a DataFrame pipe

dbt.note: Managed by dbt, no explicit ports

external.note: Declared externally, downstream can depend on it but nothing feeds into it

resourceProviderContract.description: Contract governing how resource-providing components declare and how the designer consumes them.

designerBehavior.resourcePickerPopulation: resource-picker dropdowns aggregate from two sources: (1) existing resources returned by GET /api/resources (defined in user code), (2) x-dagster-provides values from all components currently placed in the project canvas
designerBehavior.graphEdges: If component A declares x-dagster-provides: ['my_resource'] and component B has resource_key: 'my_resource', draw a dependency edge A → B in the resource graph view
designerBehavior.validation: If a component's resource_key value does not match any x-dagster-provides in the project AND does not appear in GET /api/resources, show a warning: 'Resource my_resource is not defined. Add a resource component or define it in code.'
designerBehavior.resourceTab: The Resources tab lists all resources from both sources: code-defined (from API) and component-provided (from x-dagster-provides on canvas). Component-provided resources show 'via <ComponentName>' and link to that component's config.

example.resourceKey: Must exactly match the string declared in x-dagster-provides

categories.resource: All components in the resources/ top-level directory are resource-providing by convention
